article_title: Upside Robotics Raises $7.5 Million to Scale Autonomous Robots That Cut Corn Fertilizer Use by 70 Percent

content_preview.title: Upside Robotics Raises $7.5 Million to Scale Autonomous Robots That Cut Corn Fertilizer Use by 70 Percent
content_preview.summary: Canadian startup Upside Robotics closes a $7.5 million seed round to expand solar-powered autonomous robots that micro-dose fertilizer in corn fields, claiming 70 percent input reduction across 1,300 acres.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Upside Robotics, a Waterloo, Ontario-based startup building autonomous solar-powered robots for precision fertilizer application, has raised $7.5 million in seed funding to scale its operations across Canada and into the United States Corn Belt. The round was led by Plural, with participation from Garage Capital and the founders of Clearpath Robotics, bringing the company's total funding to more than $11 million, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/upside-rob...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-balanced agtech startup article with strong counterpoint from Inside Climate News investigation. Appropriately skeptical of self-reported claims.
editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch confirms $7.5M seed, Plural led, $11M total, Dugan/Tian founders, 70% reduction, $150/acre, 10K km, 100K liters, 1300 acres, 200 waitlist, 100% retention. Inside Climate News confirms $30B market, N2O 40% rise, 265x warming, sustainability claims questioned. EPA confirms nutrient runoff as major pollution source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Minor note: article says Clearpath Robotics founders participated but TechCrunch says founders previously worked at Clearpath — the backing is from Clearpath founders as investors, which is consistent.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral with excellent balance between startup claims and critical scrutiny.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Upside Robotics or precision fertilizer robots.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong agtech article with verified claims and balanced reporting. Approved.
